breakdown: This PR refactors the rewards box normalization to use a centralized robust numeric converter with fallback key chains. It introduces a _get_raw helper that intelligently searches multiple key aliases (xp_gained → xp → xp_awarded, etc.) while handling edge cases like empty strings, zeros, NaN, and infinity. The companion E2E test validates the robustness against LLM-generated messy numeric strings. Strengths include solid test coverage and good architectural separation; weaknesses include generic naming for internal functions and sparse inline documentation.
key_changes: Added _get_raw helper function with fallback key chain logic
Handles edge cases: empty strings, zero values, NaN/inf treated as invalid
New E2E test file validates robustness against LLM halluncinated reward fields
Test covers messy strings, fallback aliases, empty primary, and NaN scenarios
